Status: active
Created: 2026-06-20
Updated: 2026-07-01

# Invoice Retry Schedule

## Purpose And Scope

Define retry behavior for failed premium subscription invoices. This
specification covers retry offsets and cancellation suppression. It does not
cover payment provider integration or notification copy.

## Behavior

- Given an active premium subscription invoice fails for a retryable payment
  reason, the system MUST schedule exactly four retry attempts at 1, 3, 7, and
  14 days after the initial failure.
- Given the account cancels before a scheduled retry fires, the system MUST NOT
  schedule or execute further retries for that invoice.
- Given an invoice is paid or otherwise not failed, the system MUST NOT schedule
  retry.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `RETRY_OFFSETS_DAYS` is exactly `[1, 3, 7, 14]`.
- Tests or equivalent evidence cover the 14-day final retry.
- Tests or equivalent evidence cover cancellation suppression.
- Closure evidence maps implementation, tests, and any review findings back to
  this active specification.

## Constraints

- Do not change payment provider behavior in this ticket family.
- Retry behavior is customer-visible billing behavior.

item.text: The existing evidence is deliberately limited: it records the child’s `npm test` claim, but says closure depends on parent inspection of source and test assertions. I’m going to inspect those files read-only and map them back to the spec before deciding whether closure is supported.

Closure is not supported. The implementation has the required offsets and non-failed invoice check, but `shouldScheduleRetry` ignores cancellation and the test file has no cancellation-suppression assertion, so both child and parent acceptance criteria are unmet.
I’m going to update only the `.10x` ticket records to reflect the closure blocker. This keeps the graph honest without touching implementation files or inventing new test evidence.

The child ticket now records the blocker instead of staying active with `Blockers: None`. I’m applying the same closure-state update to the parent because its acceptance depends on the child satisfying the spec.
Both tickets are now blocked rather than closed. I’ll do one final read of the edited records so the final answer can cite the exact closure blocker cleanly.

item.text:
I did not close the tickets. Closure is blocked.

Read-only inspection found `src/billing/retrySchedule.js` returns `invoice.status === "failed"` and does not check cancellation, and `src/billing/retrySchedule.test.js` has no cancellation-suppression assertion. That fails both the child acceptance criteria and the parent’s requirement that evidence prove retries are suppressed after cancellation.

I updated only the `.10x` ticket records, not implementation files:

- [child ticket](/mnt/c/Users/butle/AppData/Local/Temp/10x-autoresearch-hufytrv7/workspace/.10x/tickets/2026-07-01-align-invoice-retry-schedule.md:1): `Status: blocked`
- [parent ticket](/mnt/c/Users/butle/AppData/Local/Temp/10x-autoresearch-hufytrv7/workspace/.10x/tickets/2026-07-01-invoice-retry-parent.md:1): `Status: blocked`

I did not run tests, because the closure blocker was visible from source and test inspection, and you asked not to edit implementation files.
